content: Child health encompasses physical, mental, and social well-being from birth through adolescence. Global under-5 mortality declined 59% from 1990-2021 (93 to 38 per 1,000 live births) but 5 million children still die annually, mostly from preventable causes. Leading causes: preterm birth complications, pneumonia, diarrhea, malaria, malnutrition, congenital anomalies. Social determinants profoundly affect outcomes—poverty, food insecurity, housing instability, environmental exposures, adverse childhood experiences (ACEs). Developmental milestones provide framework for monitoring growth across domains: gross/fine motor, language, cognitive, social-emotional. Critical periods include prenatal development, first 1,000 days (conception to age 2), and adolescence. Life course perspective recognizes childhood health trajectories influence adult outcomes. Equity focus essential given persistent disparities by race, ethnicity, socioeconomic status, geography, and disability.
